sections.paragraph:
only in plur. שֵׁדִים idols, pr. lords (compare בְּעָלִים), Deu 32:17 Psa 106:27 from the root שׁוּד to rule; whence سَأيِدُ, سَيِّدُ lord, master; Syriac ܫܙܺܕܐܳ demon. LXX., Vulg., δαιμόνια, dœmonia; since the Jews [rightly] regarded idols to be demons, who allowed themselves to be worshipped by men.

I. שֹׁדּ i.q. שַׁד m. breast, teat, Job 24:9 Isa 60:16 perhaps from the root שָׁדָה which see.

II.
